desc: Cairo is a high-intensity Nile megacity where Pharaonic sites, Islamic-era quarters, and modern urban life sit side by side. It's best approached with deliberate pacing: cooler-season months are strongest for major site days, while summer heat pushes activity toward early mornings and evenings.
hls: Pyramids and Sphinx on the city edge
Grand Egyptian Museum and key antiquities collections
Historic Islamic Cairo and Khan el-Khalili
Nile river setting with evening cruises

todo.name: Giza Plateau and Great Sphinx
todo.desc: Spend a half-day at the pyramids, adding interior entry or camel viewpoints if desired.

todo.name: Grand Egyptian Museum visit
todo.desc: Focus on headline treasures and Old Kingdom material with enough time for the large galleries.

todo.name: Islamic Cairo walk (al-Muizz Street)
todo.desc: Explore medieval mosques, gates, and restored facades in one of the world’s great historic urban corridors.

todo.name: Khan el-Khalili market tea stop
todo.desc: Browse brassware and textiles, then sit for mint tea in the atmospheric bazaar lanes.

todo.name: Coptic Cairo churches
todo.desc: Visit the Hanging Church and nearby religious sites that trace early Christian history in Egypt.

todo.name: Sunset felucca on the Nile
todo.desc: Take a traditional sailboat ride for breezes and skyline views away from traffic-heavy streets.

wifi.note: Major hotels and business cafés are generally workable, but performance varies by district; keep local mobile data as backup.

prac.fltNote: Gatwick to Cairo is commonly available nonstop or one-stop depending on season. Return fares are often ~£220–£420 off-peak, ~£420–£620 in shoulder demand, and ~£620+ around Easter, winter-holiday, and school-break peaks.
prac.lang: Arabic (Egyptian Arabic; English common in tourism)
prac.visa: Most visitors require an Egypt eVisa or visa on arrival; check current nationality-specific rules before travel.
alerts: Traffic can add significant transfer time — build buffers around flights and timed entries.
Summer heat can be extreme; schedule outdoor monuments early and carry water/sun protection.
Touts around major sites are common; prebook licensed guides/transport where possible.

sweet: October to April is generally the best sightseeing window for Cairo, with lower heat stress and longer comfortable walking periods.
